feedback.text: Dear Madam or Sir, I would like to express my support for the goals of the proposed Digital Fairness Act and thank the Commission for its commitment to ensuring greater consumer protection in the digital environment. In this context, I respectfully urge you to consider including the principles of the European Citizens Initiative Stop Killing Games in the final version of the legislation. This initiative, supported by over one million citizens, calls for a simple but important measure: when a video game that consumers have legally purchased is no longer supported by its publisher or platform, it should remain playable. To reflect this in the Digital Fairness Act, I propose the following considerations: Ensure continued access to purchased games Require publishers to make games playable offline or enable access through private servers when official support ends. Introduce a clear notice period Mandate that consumers receive sufficient advance notice before the removal or deactivation of digital products they have purchased. Clarify consumer rights Define that if a product becomes unusable due to a decision by the trader, it should be considered defective, giving the consumer a right to a remedy. Encourage digital sustainability and preservation Maintaining access to purchased digital content not only protects consumers but also contributes to sustainability goals and preserves digital culture. These measures would help ensure fair treatment of consumers, increase trust in digital services, and show that the EU listens to its citizens. I hope the Commission will take this opportunity to strengthen the Digital Fairness Act in line with these democratic and reasonable concerns. Thank you for considering my feedback. Kind regards, Valentin Dumitru EU citizen and software engineer
